pubmed_links: [Feinstein et al. (2014) VPS53 mutations cause progressive cerebello-cerebral atrophy type 2 (PCCA2)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24577744/); [Kaminsky et al. (2019) VPS53 gene is associated with a new phenotype of complicated hereditary spastic paraparesis](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31418091/)
summary: Biallelic mutations in VPS53, a subunit of the Golgi-associated retrograde protein (GARP) complex, cause progressive cerebello-cerebral atrophy type 2 (PCCA2) and pontocerebellar hypoplasia, characterized by severe intellectual disability, progressive microcephaly, spasticity, and early-onset epilepsy. (emerging evidence)
